Document:
# Event Flow & Lifecycle
This page covers the lifecycle events for entities and systems in OpenEdge 14.
## Entity Lifecycle Events
Entity spawned -> MapInitEvent | Components initialized -> ComponentInit | Entity ready for gameplay -> EntityInitializedMessage | (gameplay happens) | Entity marked for deletion -> EntityTerminatingEvent | Components removed -> ComponentShutdown | Entity deleted
## Key Lifecycle Events
[bold]ComponentInit[/bold] — called when a component is first added to an entity. Use for one-time setup.
[bold]MapInitEvent[/bold] — called when an entity enters the game world. Use for spawn logic.
[bold]EntityTerminatingEvent[/bold] — called before an entity is deleted. Use for cleanup.
## System Initialization
Systems initialize in the order they are registered. The [mono]Initialize()[/mono] method is called once at startup:
public override void Initialize() { base.Initialize(); SubscribeLocalEvent(OnDamage); }
## Content Under Construction
Full event lifecycle documentation coming soon. See [textlink="Event System" link="OE14_DEV_Arch_Events"] for the general event architecture.
